topic: Someone finds an unsigned birthday card in their mailbox
raw_output:
[story 1]

The mail slot clunked open, and there it was: a thick, cream-colored card, sealed with a slightly crumpled wax stamp. My hand, which had been reaching for junk mail, froze mid-air. I pulled it out, the paper feeling strangely heavy in my palm. There was no address, no name, just a beautifully scripted message wishing 'Happy Birthday' to an unnamed recipient. My eyes scanned the front, then the inside, a slow, bewildered movement. I reread the looping cursive, the sheer randomness of it hitting me like a physical blow. I blinked several times, trying to force my brain to process the impossible situation. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. I looked around my quiet living room, as if expecting someone to materialize and explain the elaborate prank.

[story 2]

I picked up the envelope, the paper cool against my fingertips. It was a birthday card, clearly intended for someone, yet utterly devoid of any identifying information. A strange tension coiled in my chest. I unfolded it, and the handwriting inside was elegant, almost calligraphic, detailing well wishes for a day that, for me, was entirely unremarkable. I held the card up to the light, scrutinizing the edges, searching for any clue, any context. My jaw went slack. I let the card slip slightly, the edges brushing against my thumb. A faint tremor ran through my hand. I stared at the blank space where a name should have been, my mind cycling through every conceivable scenario—a very elaborate joke, a misplaced item, a bizarre mystery. I stood motionless for a full minute, just breathing in, trying to anchor myself against the sudden, overwhelming sense of disorientation.

[story 3]

The sound of the mail slot opening was the only thing that broke the silence of the afternoon. I retrieved the item, and it was a card—a pristine piece of stationery bearing a cheerful birthday greeting. It was unsigned. Completely, utterly unsigned. I held it between two fingers, turning it over and over, my gaze darting from the decorative border to the simple, heartfelt message written within. A sudden, profound stillness settled over me. My posture shifted; my shoulders slumped slightly as if the weight of the discovery had suddenly become too much to bear. I let the card drop onto the wooden table, the sound barely audible. I leaned back in my chair, my eyes wide and unblinking, trying desperately to reconcile the object in my hand with the reality of my own mundane existence.
